index.title: Blog
index.description: Practical notes for Tucsenberg launch readiness: membranes, compatibility, materials, quote flow, deployment, analytics, and content boundaries.

hero.title: Replacement membrane paths for installed aeration systems.
hero.subtitle: Tucsenberg is preparing compatibility-led membrane pages for O&M teams that need OEM-family matching, material guidance, and RFQ-ready review inputs.

preview.label: What Tucsenberg is building
preview.title: Compatibility review first, broad catalog later
preview.description: The site is intentionally in a safe work-in-progress state until product data, compatibility records, and RFQ routing are owner-confirmed.
items: Membranes, compatibility, materials, and quote paths
preview.note: Owner-confirmed product data arrives in the next steps

problems.title: Replacement membrane buyers usually arrive with partial evidence, not a clean catalog SKU.
problems.description: A maintenance team may have an OEM family, an old part number, a body photo, dimensions, wastewater conditions, or only a shutdown deadline.

structure.title: No clean part path
structure.description: A buyer needs membrane diameter, diffuser body fit, OEM family, and review caveats before a quote is responsible.

content.title: Incomplete evidence
content.description: Photos, old membranes, dimensions, wastewater conditions, and quantity bands often arrive in different formats.

deployment.title: Unclear material risk
deployment.description: EPDM, TPU/PU, and later PTFE-coated EPDM need condition-based guidance instead of vague quality claims.

inquiry.title: RFQ friction
inquiry.description: Quote review is slower when part numbers, installed model, quantity, and shutdown timing are not collected together.

multilingual.title: Regional review path
multilingual.description: English and Spanish public pages must keep the same review logic while Chinese stays internal preview only.

answer.title: Tucsenberg starts with compatibility review before quote.
answer.description: The site is being shaped around part-number matching, material decision support, RFQ intake, and clear OEM trademark boundaries.

pageStructure.title: Membrane path structure
pageStructure.description: Membranes, compatibility, materials, quote, quality, procurement, and legal boundaries each have a defined place.

replacementSurface.title: Review input surface
replacementSurface.description: Part numbers, photos, dimensions, wastewater conditions, quantity bands, and shutdown timing are treated as explicit RFQ inputs.

inquiryPath.title: Quote review path
inquiryPath.description: The quote flow will ask for enough evidence to return a responsible membrane recommendation, not a brand-name match alone.

cloudflareFoundation.title: Cloudflare-ready foundation
cloudflareFoundation.description: Cloudflare/OpenNext remains the deployment path while Tucsenberg content and lead routing are confirmed.

startPath.title: A practical path from membrane evidence to RFQ.
startPath.description: Use the current placeholder IA to lock the flow, then fill it with confirmed product, compatibility, material, and quote data.

brand.title: Confirm product facts
brand.description: Membrane families, materials, legal entity, domain, contact channels, and brand assets become owner-confirmed facts.

content.title: Replace page content
content.description: Membrane pages, compatibility guides, material guidance, quality proof, images, and legal pages become Tucsenberg launch content.

forms.title: Connect quote flow
forms.description: RFQ submissions, Turnstile, lead destinations, email delivery, and response ownership point to real accounts.

deploy.title: Deploy and verify
deploy.description: Cloudflare preview, form canary, content readiness, and owner signoff prove readiness separately from local checks.

starterBoundary.title: This site is unfinished by design until Tucsenberg facts are confirmed.
starterBoundary.description: The current shell shows the intended website shape while keeping unconfirmed product data and RFQ behavior out of production claims.

items.title: Replace the business identity
items.description: Company name, domain, contact details, legal body, logo, and social links must become owner-confirmed facts.

items.title: Replace the offer story
items.description: Products, services, proof, images, and response promises must match the real business.

items.title: Connect the lead path
items.description: Inquiry forms, email, Airtable or CRM destinations, Turnstile, and canary checks must use the real environment.

items.title: Prove the deployment
items.description: Cloudflare build, preview smoke, dashboard credentials, and owner signoff stay separate from local test results.

chain.title: From part evidence to RFQ, one controlled flow.
chain.subtitle: Tucsenberg keeps compatibility review, material guidance, quote intake, and deployment proof connected while the site moves toward launch.

step1.title: Input Review
step1.desc: Clarify what part number, OEM family, photo, dimensions, or wastewater evidence the buyer has

step2.title: Content Setup
step2.desc: Replace membrane facts, compatibility paths, material guidance, proof, images, and RFQ copy in the right source files

step3.title: Component Reuse
step3.desc: Use existing sections and Storybook previews before creating new UI

step4.title: Page Assembly
step4.desc: Build a complete site from Tucsenberg sections, translations, and page content

step5.title: Verification
step5.desc: Run brand, content, component, type, test, and build checks before launch
